claim: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live enables real-time conversation with models by accepting audio, video, and desktop screen sharing as input modalities
actionable: Use Gemini 3.1 Flash Live for real-time interactive applications that require screen sharing or live video feed analysis with sub-second latency
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Paige Bailey: What's New and What's Next in AI

claim: Analyzing 5 minutes of YouTube video at 1 frame per second (sampling 39,900 tokens total) costs approximately 1.5 cents with Gemini 3.1 Flash
actionable: Use Gemini 3.1 Flash for cost-effective video analysis; budget roughly $0.30/hour of video content at current pricing for frame-by-frame processing
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Paige Bailey: What's New and What's Next in AI

claim: Google AI Studio's compare mode allows head-to-head evaluation of two different models on the same task with cost-per-request transparency
actionable: Use AI Studio's compare mode to benchmark Gemini 3.1 Flash against 3.1 Flash Light for your specific use case before production deployment
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Paige Bailey: What's New and What's Next in AI

claim: Gemma 4 open models are Apache 2 licensed and available in sizes from 2B to 31B parameters, enabling on-device and edge deployment
actionable: Deploy Gemma 4 2B for mobile devices or Gemma 4 4B for laptop deployments; use larger versions via API for more complex tasks
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Paige Bailey: What's New and What's Next in AI

claim: Free tier developers get 10,000+ daily API requests to Gemma models via Google AI Studio, enabling cost-free prototyping of multimodal tasks
actionable: Use the free tier to prototype multimodal applications with Gemma before scaling to paid Gemini APIs for production
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Paige Bailey: What's New and What's Next in AI

claim: Google AI Studio build feature allows voice-based app creation with automatic code generation, versioning, and GitHub integration for deployment
actionable: Use AI Studio build to prototype applications by describing them in voice; export code to GitHub and deploy directly to Cloud Run
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Paige Bailey: What's New and What's Next in AI

claim: Genie 3 world models enable 60-second interactive explorations of procedurally generated environments with persistent object interactions
actionable: Use Genie 3 to rapidly prototype game worlds or simulations by describing scenarios; no physics engine needed—behavior learned from training data
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Paige Bailey: What's New and What's Next in AI

claim: Lilia 3 music generation supports 30+ second audio creation and accepts custom lyrics or instrumental tracks for augmentation
actionable: Use Lilia 3 to generate background audio for video content or prototype music-driven applications; baseline can be extended with additional instrumentals
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Paige Bailey: What's New and What's Next in AI

claim: V03.1 video generation replaced a 20-minute multi-step workflow (Gemini + text-to-speech + music generation + Camtasia stitching) with <2 minutes end-to-end
actionable: Use V03.1 for rapid video content creation; generate detailed scene descriptions with Gemini 2.5 then pass to V03.1 for end-to-end production
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Paige Bailey: What's New and What's Next in AI

claim: Multimodal models (audio, video, images, text combined) achieve better performance than text-only models due to richer semantic understanding
actionable: Include multimodal examples during training or fine-tuning when available; analyze images, audio, and text together rather than in isolation
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Paige Bailey: What's New and What's Next in AI

claim: Google AI Studio's get-code feature exports all configuration, prompts, and settings to Python/TypeScript/Java so Studio experiments are reproducible
actionable: Prototype in AI Studio, use get-code to export to your codebase, then iterate locally with version control and testing frameworks
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Paige Bailey: What's New and What's Next in AI
